seoTitle: West Haven vs West Hoxton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between West Haven and West Hoxton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: West Haven vs West Hoxton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Haven and West Hoxton.
heading: West Haven vs West Hoxton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Haven, New South Wales and West Hoxton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Haven has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to West Hoxton (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Haven or West Hoxton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Haven has a flood risk score of 30/100, while West Hoxton scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Haven scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. West Hoxton scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Haven or West Hoxton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Haven has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
